checks.description: Misconfiguration: user accounts with adminCount=1 (protected object flag); review for inappropriate persistence of admin protection.
checks.remediation: Remove inappropriate adminSDHolder protection; relocate accounts to correct OUs with least-privilege delegation; investigate unexpected adminCount=1 on non-privileged users.

checks.description: Misconfiguration: security groups with adminCount=1; review membership and whether protection is expected.
checks.remediation: Validate each group is an intentional protected group; remove nested or unintended memberships; replace ad-hoc protected groups with delegated OU structure where possible.

checks.description: Misconfiguration: computer accounts with adminCount=1; validate each is an intentional protected object.
checks.remediation: Confirm Tier0/Tier1 systems only; remove adminCount=1 from non-DC servers that should not be adminSDHolder-protected; align with tiering model.

checks.description: Exposure: user accounts with sIDHistory populated (migration or attack persistence); validate legitimacy.
checks.remediation: Remove stale sIDHistory values after migration cutover; investigate unexpected values; restrict who can write sIDHistory (e.g. domain admin only).

checks.description: Misconfiguration: resource-based constrained delegation (RBCD) on computers; review msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity.
checks.remediation: Clear msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity where not required; scope principals to least privilege; monitor changes to RBCD attributes.

checks.description: Misconfiguration: RBCD on user accounts; review delegation to constrained targets.
checks.remediation: Remove RBCD from user objects unless required; prefer computer-based delegation patterns; audit who can modify these attributes.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: KeyCredentialLink on Computers. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate KeyCredentialLink on Computers per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Trust Accounts for Delegation. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Trust Accounts for Delegation per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Shortest Path to Domain Admin. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Shortest Path to Domain Admin per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration: user accounts with SPNs (Kerberoastable); review SPNs and use group managed service accounts where possible.
checks.remediation: Remove unnecessary SPNs from user accounts; prefer gMSA for services; enforce strong password policy for remaining SPN owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Shadow Credentials Detection. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Shadow Credentials Detection per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Constrained Delegation Targets. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Constrained Delegation Targets per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Resource Based Constrained Delegation. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Resource Based Constrained Delegation per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Constrained Delegation Principals. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Constrained Delegation Principals per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Admin SDHolder Template. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Admin SDHolder Template per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Privileged Access Workstation PAW Computers. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Privileged Access Workstation PAW Computers per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration: Kerberoastable user accounts (SPN on user); overlaps conceptually with ACC-034 — use for Kerberos category reporting.
checks.remediation: Remove unnecessary SPNs from user accounts; prefer gMSA for services; enforce strong password policy for remaining SPN owners (same as ACC-034).

checks.description: Misconfiguration: accounts with Kerberos pre-authentication disabled (AS-REP roastable).
checks.remediation: Enable Kerberos pre-authentication for affected accounts (or disable accounts that do not require interactive logon). Prefer group-based policies over per-account exceptions.

checks.description: Misconfiguration: non-DC computers with unconstrained delegation (TGT exposure risk).
checks.remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation or constrain to required services; treat as Tier0 adjacent.

checks.description: Misconfiguration: user accounts with unconstrained delegation enabled.
checks.remediation: Remove unconstrained delegation from user accounts unless business-critical; use constrained or resource-based delegation; treat as credential theft risk.

checks.description: Exposure: computers with constrained delegation (msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo); validate SPN targets.
checks.remediation: Restrict msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo to required SPNs; remove stale delegation; prefer resource-based delegation where possible.

checks.description: Exposure: users with constrained delegation; review allowed SPNs.
checks.remediation: Tighten allowed SPNs; remove user-based constrained delegation if not required; monitor changes to msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Protocol Transition S4U2Self. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Protocol Transition S4U2Self per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: Resource-Based Constrained Delegation. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate Resource-Based Constrained Delegation per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.

checks.description: Weak crypto: accounts allowed DES-only Kerberos (UAC bit); disable DES for the domain and clear per-account use of DES-only.
checks.remediation: Disable DES for Kerberos at domain level; clear the Use Kerberos DES encryption types only flag on accounts; migrate legacy apps off DES.

checks.description: Misconfiguration or exposure: DES-Only Kerberos Computers. Each returned object matches the LDAP rule; review for business justification and false positives.
checks.remediation: Review and remediate DES-Only Kerberos Computers per org baseline: align with tiering, least privilege, and Microsoft AD security guidance; validate with domain owners.
